query: Rivian Redwood Materials energy storage partnership announcement April 2026 details manufacturing supply chain

{"query":"Rivian Redwood Materials energy storage partnership announcement April 2026 details manufacturing supply chain","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1552,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.redwoodmaterials.com/news/rivian-and-redwood-materials-announce-energy-storage-partnership-for-manufacturing/","title":"Rivian and Redwood announce energy storage partnership","content":"First repurposed battery energy storage system for a U.S. automotive manufacturer to cut energy costs, support grid reliability.
American automotive and technology company Rivian and critical materials and energy technology company Redwood Materials today announced a partnership to deploy pioneering battery energy storage at Rivian’s Normal, Illinois manufacturing facility.
Rivian will provide EV battery packs to Redwood, who will integrate them into a Redwood Energy system, supported by the company’s Redwood Pack Manager technology, allowing their stored energy to be used on-site by Rivian’s plant in Normal."},{"url":"https://www.recyclingtoday.com/news/rivian-redwood-materials-announce-energy-storage-partnership/","title":"Rivian and Redwood Materials announce energy storage partnership - Recycling Today","content":"Using more than 100 second-life Rivian battery packs, Redwood and Rivian say the solution will initially provide 10 megawatt-hours (MWh) of dispatchable energy to reduce cost and grid load during peak demand periods, saving on cost and supporting grid security and reliability."},{"url":"https://www.wardsauto.com/news/rivian-redwood-materials-to-deploy-energy-storage-system-illinois-factory-ev-battery/817633/","title":"Rivian, Redwood Materials announce energy storage partnership | WardsAuto","content":"The EV maker’s second-life battery packs will be integrated into an energy system for its Illinois assembly plant.
“Our partnership with Rivian shows how EV battery packs can be turned into dispatchable energy resources, bringing new capacity online quickly, supporting critical manufacturing, and reducing strain on the grid without waiting years for new infrastructure,” said Redwood Materials founder and CEO JB Straubel, in a statement.
In addition, EV batteries remain healthy even when the vehicle reaches the end of its service life, so they are extremely valuable to be repurposed for stationary energy storage applications, according to Rivian.
Redwood Materials was founded in 2017 by Straubel, a co-founder of Tesla and its former CTO.
There is just one standard internal-combustion-engine winner on our 2025 Wards 10 Best Engines & Propulsion Systems' winners list, with the rest employing either full or partial electrification."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rivian_rivian-adventurousforever-energystorage-activity-7450209318932766720-4LQw","title":"Rivian Partners with Redwood Materials for Second-Life Battery Storage | Rivian posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"This partnership gives a valuable energy resource a new life.
By 2040, repurposed second-life EV batteries alone could theoretically supply over 250 GWh of capacity annually.
This is enough to meet the entire U.S. grid storage installation need with zero new manufacturing, keeping tens of billions of dollars entirely within the domestic economy.
In partnership with Redwood Materials, we are partnering to deploy a 10 MWh energy storage system at our Normal, Illinois manufacturing plant.
Rivian just partnered with Redwood Materials, co-founded by Tesla's JB Straubel, to take end-of-life EV battery packs and repurpose them as a 10 MWh energy storage system for their Illinois factory.
Rivian is partnering with Redwood Materials to install a second-life battery energy storage system at its Illinois plant, using over 100 used Rivian vehicle battery packs."},{"url":"https://www.rivianforums.com/rivian-and-redwood-materials-partner-to-re-use-rivian-batteries-at-normal-plant/","title":"Rivian and Redwood Materials partner to re-use Rivian batteries at Normal plant | Rivian Forums - R1 R2 R3 R4 News, Specs, Models, RIVN Stock -- Rivianforums.com","content":"2023 El Cap Quad Motor R1T (Selling the Xterra)
Not sure if this announcement involves any true recycling or if it's just straight-up repurposing the packs..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Rivian Redwood Materials energy storage partnership announcement April 2026 details manufacturing supply chain","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.redwoodmaterials.com/news/rivian-and-redwood-materials-announce-energy-storage-partnership-for-manufacturing","title":"Rivian and Redwood announce energy storage partnership","content":"# Rivian and Redwood Materials announce energy storage partnership for manufacturing. American automotive and technology company Rivian and critical materials and energy technology company Redwood Materials today announced a partnership to deploy pioneering battery energy storage at Rivian’s Normal, Illinois manufacturing facility. Using more than 100 second-life Rivian battery packs, Redwood and Rivian’s solution will initially provide 10 megawatt-hours (MWh) of dispatchable energy to reduce cost and grid load during peak demand periods—saving on cost and supporting grid security and reliability. Rivian will provide EV battery packs to Redwood, who will integrate them into a Redwood Energy system, supported by the company’s Redwood Pack Manager technology, allowing their stored energy to be used on-site by Rivian’s plant in Normal. Our partnership with Rivian shows how EV battery packs can be turned into dispatchable energy resources, bringing new capacity online quickly, supporting critical manufacturing, and reducing strain on the grid without waiting years for new infrastructure.","score":0.903098,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/04/14/rivian-redwood-materials-energy-storage-partnership-illinois","title":"Rivian partners with Tesla co-founder's Redwood on energy storage | Electrek","content":"# Rivian partners with Tesla co-founder’s Redwood on energy storage. Rivian is partnering with Redwood Materials, the battery company founded by Tesla co-founder JB Straubel, to build a second-life battery energy storage system at its EV manufacturing plant in Normal, Illinois. The two companies announced the tie-up on Monday, billing it as the first repurposed battery energy storage system deployed at a U.S. automaker’s manufacturing facility. Rivian is supplying the EV battery packs, and Redwood is integrating them into a Redwood Energy system running on its in-house Redwood Pack Manager software, which is designed to control packs of mixed chemistries and states of health as a single dispatchable asset. The initial 10 MWh installation is modest compared to Redwood’s flagship project — a 12 MW / 63 MWh microgrid built from 792 second-life EV packs at its Sparks, Nevada campus to power a Crusoe AI data center — but it’s the first time Redwood has plugged its energy storage product directly into an auto plant.","score":0.89501935,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.recyclingtoday.com/news/rivian-redwood-materials-announce-energy-storage-partnership","title":"Rivian and Redwood Materials announce energy storage partnership - Recycling Today","content":"# Rivian and Redwood Materials announce energy storage partnership. Rivian will provide EV battery packs to Redwood, who will integrate them into a Redwood Energy system. Redwood Materials, a critical materials and energy technology company headquartered in Nevada, has partnered with Rivian, an American automotive and technology company, to deploy pioneering battery energy storage at Rivian’s Normal, Illinois, manufacturing facility. Using more than 100 second-life Rivian battery packs, Redwood and Rivian say the solution will initially provide 10 megawatt-hours (MWh) of dispatchable energy to reduce cost and grid load during peak demand periods, saving on cost and supporting grid security and reliability. Rivian will provide electric vehicle (EV) battery packs to Redwood, who will integrate them into a Redwood Energy system supported by the company’s Redwood Pack Manager technology, allowing their stored energy to be used on-site at Rivian’s plant in Normal. Our partnership with Redwood enables us to utilize our vehicle’s batteries beyond the life of a vehicle and contribute to grid health and American competitiveness.”.","score":0.8573986,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://x.com/SawyerMerritt/status/2044092892763873403?lang=en","title":"Rivian and Redwood Materials today announced a partnership to deploy ...","content":"NEWS: Rivian and Redwood Materials today announced a partnership to deploy battery energy storage at Rivian's Normal, Illinois manufacturing","score":0.7610953,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rivian_rivian-adventurousforever-energystorage-activity-7450209318932766720-4LQw","title":"Rivian Partners with Redwood Materials for Second-Life Battery ...","content":"Rivian is partnering with Redwood Materials to install a second-life battery energy storage system at its Illinois plant, using over 100 used","score":0.6356076,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.65,"request_id":"00e1b45b-2d34-4b5b-aec2-4290d1e6ebd8"}
